full_prompt:
Design a machined aluminum 6061-T6 gearbox housing with all non-individually-toleranced dimensions governed by ISO 2768-mK (medium linear + K geometric class), per ISO 2768-1:1989 Table 1 and ISO 2768-2:1989 Table 1. The housing contains two bearing bores that hold a single-stage spur-gear reduction; the bearing bores and gear-mesh center distance ARE individually toleranced (not ISO 2768) to IT7 / h7 fits and thus fall outside the general-tolerance pass/fail for this brief. All other dimensions (envelope, rib thicknesses, non-functional external surfaces, mounting-hole center-to-center, cosmetic chamfers, flatness of outside faces) are ISO 2768-mK.

Geometry. Housing envelope 220 mm x 160 mm x 110 mm, wall thickness 6 mm nominal, top lid joint plane through the gear centerline. Two bearing bores 72 mm H7 ID (individually toleranced, not ISO 2768) with shoulders 10 mm deep. Mounting flange 220 mm x 160 mm with four M10 through holes on a 180 mm x 120 mm pattern (center-to-center dimensioning falls under ISO 2768-m for this brief - see R2). Four internal ribs 5 mm x 20 mm stiffen the bearing-bore shoulders. External surface finishes: Ra 3.2 micron mill finish.

Material. Al 6061-T6, E = 68.9 GPa, nu = 0.33, rho = 2700 kg/m^3, Fty = 276 MPa, Ftu = 310 MPa. For structural check only; the primary pass/fail is tolerance compliance.

ISO 2768-m linear tolerance table applied to non-individually-toleranced linear dimensions:
- 0.5 to 3 mm: +/- 0.1 mm
- over 3 to 6 mm: +/- 0.1 mm
- over 6 to 30 mm: +/- 0.2 mm
- over 30 to 120 mm: +/- 0.3 mm
- over 120 to 400 mm: +/- 0.5 mm
- over 400 to 1000 mm: +/- 0.8 mm

ISO 2768-m angular tolerance:
- short side <= 10 mm: +/- 1 degree
- over 10 to 50 mm: +/- 30 minutes
- over 50 to 120 mm: +/- 20 minutes

ISO 2768-2 K class geometric tolerances:
- straightness/flatness up to 10 mm: 0.05 mm; up to 30: 0.1; up to 100: 0.2; up to 300: 0.4; up to 1000: 0.6
- perpendicularity up to 100 mm: 0.4; up to 300: 0.6; up to 1000: 0.8
- symmetry/runout up to 100 mm: 0.6; etc.

Load cases (tolerance + minor structural). LC1 (tolerance compliance, full manufacturing dimensional stack): every non-individually-toleranced dimension must fall within its ISO 2768-mK tolerance band. LC2 (structural under bearing loads): radial load 2500 N at each bearing bore, axial 500 N, with mounting flange fully fixed. Compute max von Mises in housing walls; not a precision test but retained for design credibility.

Pass/fail criteria. (R1) Envelope faces flatness over 220 mm must be <= 0.4 mm (ISO 2768-K from 100 to 300 mm). (R2) Mounting-hole center-to-center at 180 mm nominal: +/- 0.5 mm per ISO 2768-m linear (120-400 mm band). (R3) Rib thickness 5 mm nominal: +/- 0.1 mm (ISO 2768-m linear, 3-6 mm band). (R4) External corner chamfer angles nominally 45 degrees on edges <= 50 mm long: +/- 30 minutes per ISO 2768-m angular (10-50 mm band). (R5) Top lid joint plane perpendicularity to mounting-face reference A: <= 0.6 mm over 160 mm (ISO 2768-K perpendicularity 100-300 mm band). (R6) Structural: max von Mises under LC2 bearing loads must not exceed Fty / 1.5 = 184 MPa (generic 1.5 structural FoS, not from ISO 2768). (R7) Housing mass <= 3.0 kg. (R8) All individually toleranced dimensions (bearing bores 72 H7, gear center distance 90 +/- 0.02) must be called out separately on the drawing and must NOT default to ISO 2768 (meta-check).

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: a CAD-native tolerance audit (PMI / GD&T check script) comparing nominal geometry to ISO 2768-mK tolerance bands on all non-individually-toleranced dimensions. Secondary: linear static FEM for LC2 structural confirmation. GD&T profile of a surface checks require either NX/CATIA PMI validation or a toleranced GD&T spreadsheet. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 with PMI annotations including one ISO 2768-mK title-block callout (e.g. 'Linear and angular tolerances per ISO 2768-m; geometric per ISO 2768-K unless otherwise specified'), a tolerance-audit report listing every non-individually-toleranced dimension and its ISO 2768-m allowed band, and the LC2 structural von Mises report.
short_prompt:
Design a machined 6061-T6 gearbox housing with all non-individually-toleranced dimensions governed by ISO 2768-mK (ISO 2768-1 + ISO 2768-2, 1989). The two bearing bores (72 H7) and the gear-mesh center distance (90 +/- 0.02) are individually toleranced and must be called out separately on the drawing.

Envelope: 220 x 160 x 110 mm housing with 6 mm nominal wall, mounting flange 220 x 160 mm with 4 M10 through-holes on a 180 x 120 mm pattern, four 5 x 20 mm internal ribs, and top lid joint plane through the gear centerline. External surface Ra 3.2 um.

Load cases.
- LC1 tolerance compliance: every non-individually-toleranced linear, angular, and geometric dimension must fall inside the ISO 2768-mK band for its size range (linear tables in ISO 2768-1; flatness/perpendicularity from ISO 2768-2 K class).
- LC2 bearing-load structural check: 2,500 N radial + 500 N axial at each bearing bore with the mounting flange fully fixed.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Primary pass/fail is dimensional-tolerance compliance, not FEM.

prompt.title: Gearbox housing with ISO 2768-mK general tolerances
prompt.brief: 220x160x110 mm 6061-T6 gearbox housing; linear + angular + geometric tolerances default to ISO 2768-mK on all non-individually-toleranced features; bearing bores IT7/h7 individually toleranced.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Bearing bores + gear center distance must NOT default to ISO 2768

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Surface roughness functional specs beyond Ra 3.2
Fastener-hole positional tolerances for gasketed joints
